rationale: The citing paper discusses Okada (2005) in the theory section as key prior evidence on preference reversals between hedonic and utilitarian options across choice versus evaluation settings. It uses that evidence to motivate H7 and to define a specific gap: prior designs changed both presentation mode and elicitation mode, so the causal role of elicitation mode alone was unclear. In Experiment 3 and the general discussion, the authors explicitly position their contribution against Okada by showing reversals when only elicitation mode is varied, with an added emotion-mediation account.
relation_description: The citing study builds directly on Okada (2005)'s reversal finding and extends it by isolating mode of preference elicitation as sufficient to produce the reversal under joint presentation. It also adds a proposed emotional mechanism for why the reversal occurs.
